message:
perf(io): optimize file I/O for large files and command output

Two performance improvements for I/O-heavy operations:

internal/context/history.go - ReadRecent() optimization:
- Problem: Previously read entire file then sliced last N entries,
  which is O(n) for all entries regardless of how few are requested.
- Solution: Scan backwards from end of file looking for newlines,
  then seek to that position and read only the needed entries.
- Uses 4KB buffer chunks to scan backwards efficiently.
- Falls back to reading from start if fewer than N entries exist.
- Also adds proper scanner buffer sizing (64KB initial, 5MB max).
- Result: ReadRecent(10) on 100K entry file is now O(1) not O(100K).

internal/tools/s2p.go - Output buffer limiting:
- Problem: Large s2p outputs could consume unbounded memory if a
  project has many files or deep directory structures.
- Solution: Implement LimitedBuffer that errors on overflow.
- Sets 10MB limit on s2p output to prevent OOM conditions.
- Returns clear error message when limit is exceeded.
- Allows graceful degradation instead of crash.
